data.description:
Mirror of GitHub issue #805 (https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/issues/805).

Build the orchestrator the Data Source registry (#756) was designed for but deferred. Per the Data Source Hierarchy spec (#732, "Add non-Exclusions to the Data Source Registry"): a new system_process after external_community_engagement_check that calls ordered non-exclusion sources, stops on the first positive result, and takes over the negative-determination + report_activities handoff from the community-engagement check.

No real non-exclusion source exists yet, so this is orchestrator-only, proven against a mock source behind a feature flag (off in production, so behavior is unchanged).

## Acceptance Criteria
- New system_process after external_community_engagement_check; iterates enabled sources with an order (ascending), stops on first :success. order: nil sources are skipped.
- Records the producing source id for the API outcome; :skipped/:error do not stop the pass.
- Owns the negative-determination + report_activities trigger when no source succeeds; the community-engagement check routes not-met members into the orchestrator.
- Mock Verification::DataSource registered only when FEATURE_MOCK_DATA_SOURCES is on; add that flag to Features::FEATURE_FLAGS (default false).
- Automated tests via mock adapters: ordering, stop-on-success, skip/error, empty-set, provenance.

## Out-of-scope
- Exclusion ranking (Exclusion.priority_order).
- Migrating existing exception/CE determinations into registered adapters.
- Hybrid (exclusion + non-exclusion) sources.

## Coordination
- Merge order vs #767 (same business process, also adds mock sources); consider a shared FEATURE_MOCK_DATA_SOURCES flag.

## Links
- Epic #732; registry #755/#756; related #767/#768
data.title: Orchestrate non-exclusion verification data sources in priority order
